summary:
Prostate cancer is the primary source of osteoblastic secondary bone metastases due to its strong link with widespread skeletal involvement involving significant new bone formation; among all listed malignancies, this pattern occurs most frequently and prominently in advanced or hormone-refractory settings.

The summary above captures only essential facts related to which tumor causes osteoblastic secondaries without adding extra details beyond what was discussed during analysis.

**Corrected Summary:**  
Prostate cancer is the leading cause of osteoblastic bone metastases, affecting approximately 90% of men with castration-resistant disease due to pronounced stimulation of bone-forming cells. This makes it significantly more likely than breast, lung, or bladder tumors to produce characteristic osteoblastic secondaries in the skeleton.
